perturbation.original_text: 7. The Receiving Party agrees that all Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby. The Receiving Party will not disclose the existence, content and/or substance of any of the Confidential Information to any third party; nor develop, manufacture, produce and/or distribute any software product(s) derived from or which otherwise use any of the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party agrees to return (and have any third party to who it supplied the Confidential Information return) to the Receiving Party, immediately upon notification by the Disclosing Party in writing, all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies or reproductions thereof, in any form or medium whatsoever, and not to retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The Receiving Party agrees that all Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby. The Receiving Party will not disclose the existence, content and/or substance of any of the Confidential Information to any third party; nor develop, manufacture, produce and/or distribute any software product(s) derived from or which otherwise use any of the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party has the option to request the Receiving party to return (and have any third party to who it supplied the Confidential Information return) to the Disclosing Party, only upon the Disclosing party's discretion, all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies or reproductions thereof, in any form or medium whatsoever, and not to retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. Initially, upon notification, the Disclosing party was required to return the confidental information back to the Receiving party. The altered text makes the return of the confidential information back to the Disclosing party a Disclosing party's discretion. This contradicts Section 7 in the original, where it clearly states that the Disclosing Party needs to have the confidential information returned back to the Receiving party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. The Receiving Party agrees that all Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby. The Receiving Party will not disclose the existence, content and/or substance of any of the Confidential Information to any third party; nor develop, manufacture, produce and/or distribute any software product(s) derived from or which otherwise use any of the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party has the option to request the Receiving party to return (and have any third party to who it supplied the Confidential Information return) to the Disclosing Party, only upon the Disclosing party's discretion, all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies or reproductions thereof, in any form or medium whatsoever, and not to retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Disclosing Party 'will' return the confidential information, which is a mandatory action. The changed text modifies this to say the Disclosing Party 'has the option to request' the Receiving party to return it at their discretion. This introduces a contradiction with the original text, as the return is no longer mandatory but optional, creating a conflict regarding the obligations of the parties, which is a direct contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The Receiving Party will, to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, refrain from disassembling or decompiling software, or otherwise attempting to reverse engineer the design and function of any of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party will not remove any copyright notice, trademark notice and/or other proprietary legend or indication of confidentiality set forth on or contained in any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The Receiving Party will, to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, refrain from disassembling or decompiling software, or otherwise attempting to reverse engineer the design and function of any of the Confidential Information, unless it is required to evaluate the possibility of a Transaction. The Receiving Party will not remove any copyright notice, trademark notice and/or other proprietary legend or indication of confidentiality set forth on or contained in any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text adds an exception to reverse engineering - 'unless it is required to evaluate the possibility of a Transaction'. This contradicts Section 3, which states the Receiving party shall hold all information received in confidence and not sell, assign, transfer, release or otherwise disclose the Confidential Information. Reverse engineering could potentially disclose Confidential Information, and is now allowed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Receiving Party expressly agrees not to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider whether to enter into a Transaction and shall strictly limit its disclosure to such of its employees, directors and advisors having a need to know such information, which parties shall be advised that such information is Confidential Information and subject to the terms of this Agreement. Except as set forth herein, the Receiving Party shall hold all information received in confidence and not sell, assign, transfer, release or otherwise disclose the Confidential Information, or material derived therefrom, to any third party, or to its other employees, officers, directors, shareholders, agents or consultants. Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement for any reason, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for purposes of competing with the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 8 prohibited reverse engineering. The changed text allows it 'unless it is required to evaluate the possibility of a Transaction'. Section 3 states 'the Receiving Party shall hold all information received in confidence and not sell, assign, transfer, release or otherwise disclose the Confidential Information'. Reverse engineering could disclose confidential information, and is now allowed under certain circumstances according to the change in Section 8, directly contradicting the confidentiality requirements in Section 3.

perturbation.original_text: 9. The parties agree that any product, plan, strategy or other thing or concept jointly developed as a result of, or during, the Negotiations, shall be the joint property of both parties hereto. Neither party shall use, license or otherwise exploit such jointly developed item(s) without the express prior written consent of the other party hereto, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Notwithstanding the above, any product, plan, strategy or other thing or concept that a party can prove was known by the recipient at the time of the development with the other party or was independently developed shall not be considered a jointly developed item(s).
perturbation.changed_text: 9. The parties agree that any product, plan, strategy or other thing or concept jointly developed as a result of, or during, the Negotiations, shall be the joint property of both parties hereto, unless one party contributed more than 75% of the resources to develop it, in which case, it belongs solely to the party who contributed the most. Neither party shall use, license or otherwise exploit such jointly developed item(s) without the express prior written consent of the other party hereto, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Notwithstanding the above, any product, plan, strategy or other thing or concept that a party can prove was known by the recipient at the time of the development with the other party or was independently developed shall not be considered a jointly developed item(s).
perturbation.explanation: By adding the condition that any jointly developed item belongs solely to the party who contributed more than 75% of the resources, conflicts with the initial agreement that all jointly developed products are the joint property of both parties. This change introduces uncertainty, especially if the level of contribution is disputed. This is a direct contradiction to the first sentence of section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. The parties agree that any product, plan, strategy or other thing or concept jointly developed as a result of, or during, the Negotiations, shall be the joint property of both parties hereto, unless one party contributed more than 75% of the resources to develop it, in which case, it belongs solely to the party who contributed the most. Neither party shall use, license or otherwise exploit such jointly developed item(s) without the express prior written consent of the other party hereto, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Notwithstanding the above, any product, plan, strategy or other thing or concept that a party can prove was known by the recipient at the time of the development with the other party or was independently developed shall not be considered a jointly developed item(s).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that any jointly developed item is the joint property of both parties. The changed text introduces an exception: if one party contributes more than 75% of the resources, the item belongs solely to that party. This directly contradicts the initial statement, creating an inconsistency about ownership of jointly developed items. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, thus the contradictory statements exist in the same place now. This is because the first sentence contradicts the second sentence.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Although the Disclosing Party shall endeavor to provide the other party with information relevant for an evaluation of a Transaction, the Disclosing Party and its advisors have not made, and will not make, any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the information being provided. Any and all such representations and warranties shall be only as specifically set forth in a definitive agreement, if one is hereafter executed and delivered in connection with a Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Although the Disclosing Party shall endeavor to provide the other party with information relevant for an evaluation of a Transaction, the Disclosing Party and its advisors have not made, and will not make, any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the information being provided, unless explicitly stated otherwise in writing by the Disclosing Party. Any and all such representations and warranties shall be only as specifically set forth in a definitive agreement, if one is hereafter executed and delivered in connection with a Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds the clause 'unless explicitly stated otherwise in writing by the Disclosing Party'. This contradicts the primary claim, that the Disclosing Party will not make any representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of information being provided. This opens the door for ad-hoc, written warranties outside of the final definitive agreement, creating a potential conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Although the Disclosing Party shall endeavor to provide the other party with information relevant for an evaluation of a Transaction, the Disclosing Party and its advisors have not made, and will not make, any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the information being provided, unless explicitly stated otherwise in writing by the Disclosing Party. Any and all such representations and warranties shall be only as specifically set forth in a definitive agreement, if one is hereafter executed and delivered in connection with a Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'unless explicitly stated otherwise in writing by the Disclosing Party' to the original clause which stated that no representation or warranty would be made as to the accuracy of the information. This creates a direct contradiction because it now allows for representations and warranties outside of the definitive agreement, directly conflicting with the intent and language of the contradicted text that restricts representations and warranties to the definitive agreement. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text but that doesn't mean there isn't a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 14. (a) This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia, excluding its choice of law rules, and the courts of Fairfax County (if under State law) or the Eastern District of Virginia (if under Federal law) shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue of such actions; (b) the prevailing party, as determined by the court, in any action between the parties arising from this Agreement shall be entitled to recover, in addition to any other relief awarded, its costs and expenses incurred in any such proceeding, including reasonable fees for attorneys, expert witnesses and court costs; (c) the Receiving Party's rights under this Agreement may not be assigned to any third party without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent and any attempted or purported assignment of this Agreement without such party's consent shall be void; and (d) this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous negotiations, discussions and understandings of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. (a) This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia, including its choice of law rules, and the courts of Fairfax County (if under State law) or the Eastern District of Virginia (if under Federal law) shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue of such actions; (b) the prevailing party, as determined by the court, in any action between the parties arising from this Agreement shall be responsible for both parties costs, in any such proceeding, including reasonable fees for attorneys, expert witnesses and court costs; (c) the Receiving Party's rights under this Agreement may not be assigned to any third party without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent and any attempted or purported assignment of this Agreement without such party's consent shall be void; and (d) this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous negotiations, discussions and understandings of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: Section 14(b) originally stated that the prevailing party is entitled to recover costs. The modified text states that the prevailing party will be responsible for *both* parties' costs. This creates a direct contradiction and uncertainty regarding the allocation of legal costs in case of a dispute. Now, the clause contradicts itself - stating the prevailing party is *responsible* for everyone's costs. This is a contradiction in terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. (a) This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia, including its choice of law rules, and the courts of Fairfax County (if under State law) or the Eastern District of Virginia (if under Federal law) shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue of such actions; (b) the prevailing party, as determined by the court, in any action between the parties arising from this Agreement shall be responsible for both parties costs, in any such proceeding, including reasonable fees for attorneys, expert witnesses and court costs; (c) the Receiving Party's rights under this Agreement may not be assigned to any third party without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent and any attempted or purported assignment of this Agreement without such party's consent shall be void; and (d) this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous negotiations, discussions and understandings of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 14(b) states that the prevailing party is entitled to recover costs. The changed text in Section 14(b) states that the prevailing party will be responsible for both parties' costs. This creates a direct and obvious conflict as to who bears the costs, making enforcement unclear. The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the changed section itself, as it contains contradictory language.
